explanation.sourceText: The English term 'mother-in-law' is a compound phrase combining 'mother' with the relational preposition 'in-law', indicating a family connection by marriage.
explanation.targetText: Termenul englezesc „mother-in-law” este o expresie compusă care combină „mother” cu prepoziția relațională „in-law”, indicând o legătură de familie prin căsătorie.

target.definition: Mama soțului sau a soției, Mutter des Ehepartners.

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian word 'soacră' derives from the Latin 'socrus', meaning 'mother-in-law', which entered Romanian through Vulgar Latin.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul românesc „soacră” provine din latină, de la „socrus”, care însemna „mama soțului” și a pătruns în română prin latina vulgară.

examples.sourceText: My mother-in-law is coming to visit tomorrow.
examples.targetText: Soacra mea vine mâine în vizită.

examples.sourceText: The mother-in-law often helps with the household chores.
He has a good relationship with his mother-in-law.
examples.targetText: El are o relație bună cu soacra lui.

examples.sourceText: The mother-in-law is cooking for the whole family today.
examples.targetText: Soacra gătește astăzi pentru toată familia.

learningTips.text: Remember 'soacră' sounds like 'soak-ruh'—imagine your mother-in-law soaking up all the family stories.
The ending '-că' in 'soacră' is a common feminine suffix in Romanian, similar to '-ess' in English (like 'actress').
Think of 'soacră' as related to the Latin 'socrus', which also gave Italian 'suocera'—all meaning mother-in-law.
Picture your 'soacră' as the sun ('soar' sound) shining warmly over the family, holding everyone together.
